mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and higher liquidity conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also declining, confirming that easing is not being offset by an inflation shock.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing observations do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL is rising, indicating the rate cut channel is not valuation-supportive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor market deterioration and weaker future household income, which reduces risk appetite and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal is most informative when PAYEMS is also slowing, because job losses then reflect broad demand weakening rather than noise.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over three months and PAYEMS growth is decelerating, or if the conditional rank correlation is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and near-term revenue momentum for consumer-facing firms, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should appear as a short-horizon continuation signal from improving domestic consumption.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX direction is not positive in at least 55% of months with rising RSAFS, or if the relationship disappears in rolling 3-year windows despite continued RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and earnings pressure for cyclicals, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is clearest when HOUST also weakens, because the decline then reflects a broad supply-side slowdown rather than sector-specific noise.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO trend episodes do not correspond to below-median 20d SPX returns, or if the signal fails when HOUST is simultaneously declining, implying no broad supply slowdown linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST indicates improving housing demand and associated wealth effects, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal should strengthen when GDPC1 is also expanding, because housing gains then sit inside a broader demand upswing.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-ahead SPX returns are not positive in a majority of housing-upturn episodes, or if the effect vanishes when GDPC1 is not also improving.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level tightens financial conditions and lowers the present value of future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon effect should be strongest when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because real policy restraint is then more restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX does not fall over the next 5 trading days in more than half of high-rate observations, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is high, showing no restrictive real-rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS captures payroll contraction and weaker labor income, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next month as investors price slower consumption and profit growth. The signal fails when payroll weakness is offset by stable unemployment, because then the move is too small to indicate broad labor damage.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll decline episodes do not produce negative 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if unemployment stability does not matter for signal strength.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad final demand acceleration, which should raise earnings expectations and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The hypothesis is falsified if GDP growth alone works without retail strength, because then the mechanism is not demand breadth but a narrower statistical artifact.
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-ahead SPX returns are not positive when both GDPC1 and RSAFS improve together, or if GDPC1 alone explains the effect without RSAFS confirming breadth.
